title: The Sumerian King List: Before the Flood, Kingship Descended from Heaven
description: The Sumerian King List — a list of rulers from the beginning of time, starting with kings who reigned for tens of thousands of years before the Flood, then increasingly normal lifespans after it. It parallels the long lifespans in Genesis 5 and the transition after Noah's flood.
introduction:
The Sumerian King List, best preserved on the Weld-Blundell Prism (c. 2100 BCE), records a continuous sequence of rulers from the moment 'kingship descended from heaven' through historical times. Its most remarkable feature is the division of history into two periods: before and after the Flood.

Before the Flood, eight kings ruled five cities for a combined total of 241,200 years. After the Flood, reign lengths gradually decrease from thousands of years to historically plausible figures. The parallel to Genesis 5 (where pre-Flood patriarchs live 700-900 years, declining after Noah) is one of the most discussed connections between Mesopotamian and biblical chronology.

translation.text: When kingship descended from heaven, the kingship was in Eridu. In Eridu, Alulim became king; he ruled for 28,800 years. Alalgar ruled for 36,000 years. Two kings; they ruled for 64,800 years. Then Eridu fell and the kingship was taken to Bad-tibira. In Bad-tibira, En-men-lu-ana ruled for 43,200 years. Then the flood swept over. After the flood had swept over, and the kingship had descended from heaven, the kingship was in Kish. In Kish, Ga-ur became king; he ruled for 1,200 years. After the flood, the lengths of reigns become shorter, gradually approaching historical norms.
